summary: Iteration 39 - Testing 7 NEW NOC platform features. Backend: 24/24 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All 7 new pages verified working. Fixed route ordering bug in bandwidth API. All features functional.

backend_api: GET /api/trends/{client_id}?days=7 - Returns VA score trend, availability trend, alert trend data (200 OK)
GET /api/maintenance/{client_id} - Returns maintenance windows list (200 OK)
POST /api/maintenance/{client_id} - Creates new maintenance window (200 OK)
GET /api/correlation/{client_id} - Returns correlation analysis with patterns (200 OK)
POST /api/bandwidth/process-poll - Receives bandwidth data from connector (200 OK)
GET /api/portal/{client_id} - Returns client portal data WITHOUT auth (PUBLIC endpoint, 200 OK)
frontend_ui: TrendPage loads at /trends with 4 chart panels (Disponibilita, Latenza, VA Score, Alert)
DiscoveryPage loads at /discovery with scan button and client selector
DiscoveryPage shows discovered devices with Approva/Ignora buttons
MaintenancePage loads at /maintenance with 'Nuova Finestra' button
CorrelationPage loads at /correlation with summary cards (Dispositivi, Offline, Alert Attivi, Correlazioni)
ThresholdsPage loads at /thresholds with 4 threshold groups (Connettivita, Risorse Hardware, Bandwidth, Stampanti)
BandwidthPage loads at /bandwidth with device list and interface data
BandwidthPage shows TEST_Router with GigabitEthernet0/0 interface (IN: 50.0 Mbps, OUT: 30.0 Mbps, 5.0%)
ClientPortalPage loads at /portal (PUBLIC route, no auth required)
ClientPortalPage shows client ID input and access button
ClientPortalPage displays client dashboard with stats (Dispositivi, Online, Offline, SLA, Alert)
Sidebar shows all new navigation items: Bandwidth, Grafici Trend, Manutenzione, Auto-Discovery, SOC AI Correlation, Soglie Alert

bugs_fixed.issue: Route ordering bug - /api/bandwidth/summary/{client_id} was defined AFTER /api/bandwidth/{client_id}/{device_ip}, causing 'summary' to be matched as client_id
bugs_fixed.fix: Moved /api/bandwidth/summary/{client_id} route BEFORE /api/bandwidth/{client_id}/{device_ip} so more specific route matches first

critical_code_review_comments: advanced_features.py: All 7 feature endpoints implemented correctly with proper auth (Depends(get_current_user))
advanced_features.py: Portal endpoint correctly PUBLIC (no auth dependency)
advanced_features.py: Maintenance CRUD lifecycle works correctly (create -> update -> delete)
advanced_features.py: Correlation analysis includes 5 pattern types (upstream_failure, performance_degradation, flapping, wan_failure, security_event)
advanced_features.py: Bandwidth data cleanup keeps 7 days of history
All frontend pages have proper data-testid attributes for testing
All pages use Italian language as required
Layout.js sidebar correctly shows all new navigation items in appropriate groups
seed_data_creation: Bandwidth test data created via POST /api/bandwidth/process-poll (TEST_Router with GigabitEthernet0/0)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 39 verified all 7 NEW features: Trends, Thresholds, Maintenance, Correlation, Bandwidth, Discovery, and Client Portal. Fixed route ordering bug in bandwidth API. All backend endpoints working (24/24 tests). All frontend pages accessible and functional. Portal page is PUBLIC (no auth). Maintenance CRUD lifecycle tested (create->update->delete). Bandwidth data persists in DB.
